title: A Population of Little Red Dot-like Quasars in SDSS
headline: Unveiling Little Red Dots: Compact, Red Quasars at High Redshift
tldr: Little Red Dots (LRDs) are compact, red quasars at z~5 discovered by JWST, exhibiting broad Balmer lines, weak X-rays, and SEDs that defy standard stellar or AGN models. Studying local analogs can shed light on their unusual physical processes and the early universe's quasar population.
significance: These findings challenge conventional quasar classification and suggest a previously unrecognized population of red, X-ray faint quasars in the early universe.

title: Higher Berry curvature, second Chern numbers and magnetoelectric coupling in crystalline insulators
headline: Exploring Higher Berry Curvature and Second Chern Numbers in 4D Chern Insulators
tldr: We reformulate a 4D Chern insulator as translationally invariant chains over the 3D Brillouin zone and compute its higher Berry curvature using infinite MPS. The resulting DDKS number maps the topological phase diagram of the model.
significance: This work provides a computational framework for probing higher-dimensional topological invariants in crystalline systems, bridging theory and numerical simulation.

title: Learning Action Priors for Cross-embodiment Robot Manipulation
headline: Learning Integrating Action Priors into Vision-Language Models for Cross-Embodiment Robots
tldr: Vision-Language-Action models often rely on VLM backbones, but their action modules learn motion from scratch, lacking motion priors. This paper proposes learning explicit action priors to improve cross-embodiment robot manipulation.
significance: By embedding motion priors, the approach accelerates learning and enhances generalization across different robot embodiments.

title: RevengeBench: Reverse Engineering Code-Space Policies from Behavioral Experiments
headline: Reverse Engineering Agent Code from Game Behavior
tldr: RevengeBench tests whether models can infer an agent’s underlying code solely from observed gameplay, revealing current limitations in program synthesis from behavioral data.
significance: It bridges behavioral analysis and executable code reconstruction, advancing AI interpretability and program synthesis.

title: ForceBand: Learning Forceful Manipulation with sEMG
headline: ForceBand: Low-Cost sEMG Wristband for Forceful Robot Manipulation
tldr: Human demonstration data often lacks force information, limiting robot learning of force-sensitive tasks. ForceBand provides a low-cost wrist-worn sEMG system that captures muscle activity in real time, enabling robots to learn from forceful manipulation demonstrations.
significance: By adding force sensing to motion-only demonstrations, ForceBand enables more robust and realistic robot manipulation learning.

title: On-Policy Self-Distillation with Sampled Demonstrations Reduces Output Diversity
headline: On-Policy Self-Distillation Boosts Accuracy but Lowers Output Diversity in LLMs
tldr: Using a single model as both teacher and student, on-policy self-distillation achieves high pass@1 accuracy by conditioning on correct demonstrations. However, this approach reduces rollout diversity and flattens pass@k curves, limiting gains from additional rollouts.
significance: It highlights a trade-off between accuracy and diversity in self-distillation, informing future LLM training strategies.

title: Parnassus: A GPU-enabled, Python-based Package for Fast Particle Detector Simulation and Reconstruction
headline: GPU-Accelerated Particle Detector Simulation with Parnassus: Fast, Python-Based Framework for Physics Research
tldr: Parnassus is a Python/PyTorch framework that runs on GPUs to accelerate particle detector simulation and reconstruction. It replaces costly Geant4 chains with neural or parametric models, enabling rapid, user-friendly workflows for physics experiments.
significance: By dramatically reducing simulation time, Parnassus empowers physicists to iterate designs and analyze data faster, accelerating research in high-energy and nuclear physics.

title: Simulating Universal Quantum Gate Sets on Photonic OAM Qubits: Single-Qubit and Multi-Qubit Operations via Spatial Light Modulator Phase Holography
headline: Simulating Universal Quantum Gates on Photonic OAM Qubits Using SLM Phase Holography
tldr: We present a detailed simulation framework for implementing universal quantum gates on orbital angular momentum qubits encoded in Laguerre-Gaussian beams, leveraging spatial light modulators for programmable phase holography. The study includes hardware-grounded fidelity benchmarks and demonstrates both single- and multi-qubit operations with realistic error models.
significance: This work bridges photonic quantum information processing and reconfigurable optical hardware, enabling scalable, high-fidelity quantum gate operations in a software-defined platform.

title: MVTrack4Gen: Multi-View Point Tracking as Geometric Supervision for 4D Video Generation
headline: Multi-View Point Tracking Enhances 4D Video Generation from Monocular Input
tldr: The paper introduces MVTrack4Gen, a framework that uses multi-view point tracking as geometric supervision to synthesize novel-view 4D videos from a single reference video. It overcomes the inaccuracies of traditional 3D reconstruction, achieving better geometric consistency and motion fidelity.
significance: It offers a robust alternative to explicit 3D reconstruction for dynamic scenes, enabling higher fidelity novel-view video synthesis.

title: Operational detection of Wigner negativity in arbitrary quantum states from few copies
headline: Detecting Wigner Negativity in Quantum States with Few Copies via Accessible Moments
tldr: We present a unified framework that uses experimentally measurable moments of the Wigner function to detect negativity in arbitrary quantum states. The method requires only a modest number of state copies and exploits constraints of positive phase-space distributions.
significance: This approach offers a practical, scalable tool for identifying nonclassical resources essential for quantum advantage.

title: Real-Time Voice AI Hears but Does Not Listen
headline: Real‑Time Voice AI Hears Words, Ignores Tone: A Comparative Study
tldr: We benchmark four production real‑time voice systems—GPT Realtime 2, Gemini 3.1 Flash Live, Qwen3.5 Omni Plus, and Omni Flash—on tasks that require interpreting both words and vocal delivery. All four systems respond only to the words, failing to capture the nuanced information conveyed by tone and delivery.
significance: The findings highlight a critical limitation in current real‑time voice AI, underscoring the need for models that can process paralinguistic cues.

title: DiffusionBench: On Holistic Evaluation of Diffusion Transformers
headline: DiffusionBench: A Holistic Benchmark for Diffusion Transformers Beyond ImageNet
tldr: DiffusionBench introduces a comprehensive evaluation framework for diffusion transformers, extending beyond the standard class‑conditional ImageNet setup to include text‑to‑image tasks and diverse metrics. The benchmark aims to reveal true progress in generative modeling by assessing models on multiple realistic scenarios.
significance: By providing a more realistic and multi‑faceted evaluation, DiffusionBench helps researchers gauge genuine advances in diffusion transformer capabilities rather than overfitting to a single metric.

title: The Topology of the Universe
headline: Unveiling the Universe’s Shape: How Cosmic Topology Could Reveal a Finite Cosmos
tldr: Scientists are investigating whether the universe is finite by searching for patterns in the cosmic microwave background and the distribution of galaxies. A small-scale non-trivial topology would leave detectable signatures that could challenge the assumption of infinite, homogeneous space.
significance: Discovering a finite topology would reshape our understanding of cosmology and the fundamental geometry of the universe.

title: Sonus Health: Calibrated Heart-Murmur Detection from Smartphone-Based Veterinary Auscultation
headline: Smartphone Detects Dog Heart Murmurs Early with Sonus Health
tldr: Sonus Health uses a smartphone app to capture and analyze heart sounds in dogs and cats, enabling early detection of murmurs. The system offers real-time, calibrated results that can be shared with veterinarians for prompt diagnosis.
significance: This technology empowers veterinarians to catch heart disease earlier, improving outcomes for pets.

title: InSight: Self-Guided Skill Acquisition via Steerable VLAs
headline: InSight: Autonomous Skill Learning with Steerable Vision-Language-Action Models
tldr: InSight enables vision-language-action models to autonomously acquire manipulation skills by allowing users to steer primitive actions such as moving a gripper or pouring. The framework splits learning into two stages: a skill extraction phase and a steerable action execution phase, unlocking capabilities beyond the training data.
significance: By decoupling skill acquisition from fixed training data, InSight expands the applicability of VLAs to novel tasks in real-world robotics.

title: BenchX: Benchmarking AI Models for Cancer Detection and Localization with Demographic and Protocol Biases
headline: BenchX Reveals How Demographic and Protocol Biases Skew AI Cancer Detection Accuracy
tldr: BenchX is a new benchmark that evaluates AI models for cancer detection and localization across diverse patient demographics and imaging protocols. The study shows that model performance drops significantly when applied to underrepresented groups or non-standard imaging phases.
significance: Understanding and mitigating these biases is essential for deploying reliable AI diagnostics in real-world clinical settings.

title: Queues with Correlated Service Times -- the $M/M_D/c$ Model
headline: Analyzing Multi-Server Queues with Correlated Service Times Using the M/M_D/c Model
tldr: This paper extends classic M/M/c queueing theory by incorporating correlated service times via the Marshall–Olkin multivariate exponential distribution. The resulting M/M_D/c model provides new insights into system performance and potential applications in real-time service systems.
significance: It offers a tractable framework for analyzing correlated service dynamics, improving accuracy in performance evaluation for multi-server systems.

title: FLAT: Feedforward Latent Triangle Splatting for Geometrically Accurate Scene Generation
headline: FLAT: Feedforward Latent Triangle Splatting for Accurate 3D Scene Generation
tldr: FLAT introduces a feedforward decoder that splats latent triangles to produce geometrically precise 3D scenes from a single image, surpassing prior volumetric Gaussian approaches. The method leverages diffusion models’ latent space while enabling real‑time, explorable scene rendering.
significance: It bridges the gap between high‑quality generative diffusion models and accurate geometric representations, enabling practical applications in virtual exploration and downstream vision tasks.

title: FLUX3D: High-Fidelity 3D Gaussian Generation with Diffusion-Aligned Sparse Representation
headline: FLUX3D: Diffusion-Driven Sparse Voxel Approach for High-Fidelity 3D Gaussian Splatting
tldr: FLUX3D introduces a diffusion-aligned sparse voxel representation that preserves high-frequency visual details in 3D Gaussian Splatting. By integrating diffusion models with voxel-based latent construction, the method achieves higher fidelity reconstructions compared to prior discriminative 2D feature approaches.
significance: The approach enables scalable, high-fidelity 3D scene generation from images, advancing realistic rendering and virtual environment creation.

title: Sequential Probability Ratio Test using Z-Statistics (SPRT-z): A Practical Approach for Online Experimentation
headline: SPRT-z: A Practical Sequential Test for Real-Time Online Experiments
tldr: Sequential Probability Ratio Test using Z-statistics (SPRT-z) lets practitioners stop experiments early for efficacy or futility while maintaining Type I error control. It offers a simple, online-friendly alternative to fixed-horizon testing that mitigates peeking bias.
significance: SPRT-z improves decision quality in continuous data streams by enabling early stopping without inflating false positives.

title: "Zooming In" on Agentic Web Browsers as Assistive Technologies: A Case Study with a Low-Vision Technology Expert
headline: Agentic Web Browsers: Empowering Low-Vision Users Through Conversational Web Navigation
tldr: Agentic Web Browsers (AWBs) use large language models to autonomously navigate the web, offering a conversational interface for users. In this case study, we demonstrate how AWBs can serve as assistive technology for low-vision individuals, improving accessibility and productivity.
significance: This work shows that AWBs can transform web interaction into an accessible, conversational experience for visually impaired users, highlighting their potential as scalable assistive technologies.

title: Exploring Gravitational Wave Signatures Due to Primordial Non-gaussianity and Large Scale Structure Using SKAO
headline: Probing Primordial Non-Gaussianity with SKAO's Gravitational Wave Background in the Early Universe
tldr: This chapter outlines how scalar-induced gravitational waves detectable by SKAO can reveal primordial non‑Gaussianity and large‑scale structure. By modeling the stochastic background, we show its sensitivity to small‑scale physics beyond current CMB limits.
significance: It offers a novel, high‑precision probe of early‑universe physics inaccessible to traditional cosmological observations.

title: Bounds for Standard Errors in Combined Data
headline: Bounding Standard Errors Across Samples Using Geometric Inequalities for Robust Parameter Estimation
tldr: We derive sharp lower bounds on standard errors for parameters estimated from moment conditions across multiple samples, even without correlation information. Our computationally efficient bounds extend to settings with partial correlation knowledge, enabling more reliable inference.
significance: These bounds provide practitioners with guaranteed error estimates, improving confidence in multi-sample statistical analyses.

title: AutoDex: An Automated Real-World System for Dexterous Grasping Data Collection
headline: AutoDex: Automated Real-World System for Large-Scale Dexterous Grasping Data Collection
tldr: AutoDex automates the generation and verification of dexterous grasp attempts in the real world, bridging the gap between slow teleoperation and unreliable simulation. By combining candidate generation with on‑device physical outcome recording, it produces scalable, high‑quality grasp datasets for training robust robotic manipulators.
significance: AutoDex enables rapid, bias‑free collection of real‑world grasp data, accelerating the development of reliable dexterous manipulation systems.

title: Lift4D: Harmonizing Single-View 3D Estimation for 4D Reconstruction In-the-Wild
headline: Lift4D: Merging Single-View 3D Estimation with 4D Reconstruction for Dynamic Scenes
tldr: Lift4D introduces a hybrid framework that combines direct visual cues from monocular video with learned priors to produce accurate 4D reconstructions of non-rigid objects. The method achieves real‑time performance while preserving fine geometric detail, outperforming prior end‑to‑end or deformation‑only approaches.
significance: By harmonizing data-driven priors with live observations, Lift4D enables robust, high‑fidelity 4D modeling of dynamic scenes directly from single‑view video, opening new possibilities for AR, robotics, and digital heritage.

title: Randomized YaRN Improves Length Generalization for Long-Context Reasoning
headline: Randomized YaRN Enhances Long-Context Reasoning for Large Language Models by Combining Positional Extrapolation
tldr: Randomized YaRN is a training method that improves length generalization for LLMs by integrating YaRN-based positional extrapolation with randomized positional encoding. The approach yields better performance on very long sequences, addressing a key limitation of current LLMs.
significance: This method enables LLMs to reliably handle longer contexts, expanding their applicability to complex reasoning tasks.

title: Keep The Essentials: Efficient Reference Conditioned Generation via Token Dropping
headline: Speeding Up Reference-Conditioned Diffusion: Token Dropping for Efficient Image Generation
tldr: Reference-based diffusion models allow controllable image generation but are costly when many references are used. This paper introduces token dropping to reduce runtime while preserving quality, enabling real‑time reference-conditioned synthesis.
significance: By cutting computational overhead, the method makes reference-conditioned diffusion practical for real‑time applications.

title: CoorDex: Coordinating Body and Hand Priors for Continuous Dexterous Humanoid Loco-Manipulation
headline: CoorDex: Seamless Body‑Hand Coordination for Continuous Humanoid Loco‑Manipulation Using Latent Priors
tldr: CoorDex introduces a learning pipeline that fuses high‑dimensional body and dexterous hand control into a coordinated latent space, enabling continuous locomotion and manipulation without stop‑and‑go pauses. The system leverages body and hand priors to achieve smooth, real‑time humanoid loco‑manipulation tasks.
significance: By eliminating the traditional stop‑and‑go paradigm, CoorDex paves the way for more fluid and efficient humanoid robots capable of simultaneous walking and object manipulation.

title: Semantic Browsing: Controllable Diversity for Image Generation
headline: Enhancing Diversity in Text-to-Image Models with Semantic Browsing for Creative Design Control
tldr: Modern text-to-image models produce high-fidelity images but often collapse into a single visual interpretation, limiting diversity. This paper introduces a semantic browsing approach that allows users to steer diversity through meaningful design choices, improving both variety and relevance of generated images.
significance: By enabling controllable diversity, the method expands the creative potential of diffusion-based image generation, bridging the gap between fidelity and variation.

title: AIR: Adaptive Interleaved Reasoning with Code in MLLMs
headline: AIR: Adaptive Interleaved Reasoning with Code Enhances Multimodal LLMs for Vision Perception
tldr: AIR introduces a novel framework that interleaves reasoning steps with code execution to improve multimodal LLM performance on vision tasks. By moving beyond static heuristics, it enables dynamic visual manipulation and better numerical reasoning.
significance: This approach bridges the gap between symbolic reasoning and perception, paving the way for more robust and adaptable multimodal AI systems.

title: Effective hyperuniformity in time-integrated stochastic Turing patterns
headline: Hyperuniformity Emerges in Time-Integrated Stochastic Turing Patterns
tldr: Demographic noise can generate stochastic Turing patterns even when deterministic models predict stability. By integrating over time, these patterns reveal large-scale hyperuniform organization, with variance scaling as 1/R.
significance: This work bridges stochastic pattern formation and hyperuniformity, offering a new lens to analyze noise-driven spatial order.

title: Open Problem: Is AdamW Effective Under Heavy-Tailed Noise?
headline: Is AdamW Still Optimal for Heavy-Tailed Noise in LLM Training?
tldr: AdamW dominates LLM training, but its theoretical guarantees assume finite-variance noise. Recent evidence shows heavy-tailed gradients, raising questions about its effectiveness compared to sign-based optimizers like Lion and Muon.
significance: Understanding AdamW's behavior under heavy-tailed noise is crucial for reliable and efficient LLM pretraining.

title: IMAGIN-4D: Image-Guided Controllable Interaction Generation
headline: IMAGIN-4D: Image-Guided Control for Precise Human-Object Interaction Generation
tldr: IMAGIN-4D introduces an image-guided framework that tightly couples visual cues with motion synthesis, enabling precise control over grasps, approach directions, and body poses in human-object interactions. By integrating object geometry and sparse waypoints, the method resolves underspecification issues and produces consistent, realistic interactions from text prompts.
significance: This approach advances controllable animation, robotics, and AR/VR by ensuring consistent, realistic human-object interactions from minimal input.

title: Ram-pressure signatures in the dwarf irregular galaxy SextansB revealed by deep MeerKAT HI observations
headline: MeerKAT Uncovers Ram-Pressure Effects in Dwarf Galaxy Sextans B and Its Gas
tldr: Deep MeerKAT HI observations reveal low-density intergalactic medium stripping gas from Sextans B. The study provides new insights into environmental effects on dwarf galaxies.
significance: This work demonstrates how sensitive radio observations can detect subtle ram-pressure signatures in nearby dwarf galaxies, informing models of galaxy evolution.

title: DeepSWIP: Quotient-WMC Counterfactuals for Neural Probabilistic Logic Programs
headline: DeepSWIP: Enabling Causal Counterfactual Inference in Neural Probabilistic Logic Programs
tldr: DeepSWIP introduces a single-world counterfactual semantics for DeepProbLog, allowing causal reasoning in neural probabilistic logic programs. By leveraging neural materialization, it reduces fixed-context neural predicates, enabling efficient counterfactual inference.
significance: This advances neurosymbolic AI by bridging perception, logic, and causality, facilitating more robust decision-making.

title: GPU-accelerated semidefinite programming for causal games
headline: Accelerating Quantum Causal Games with GPU-Optimized Semidefinite Programming for Efficient Simulation and Analysis
tldr: The authors present a GPU-accelerated solver for semidefinite programming that enables rapid analysis of process matrices in causal games, achieving significant speedups over CPU implementations. This approach allows large-scale exploration of quantum correlations without fixed causal order, paving the way for practical quantum advantage in distributed tasks.
significance: By providing a scalable computational framework, the work brings quantum causal analysis into the realm of practical, large-scale simulations, potentially informing future quantum communication protocols.

title: Multi-LCB: Extending LiveCodeBench to Multiple Programming Languages
headline: Multi-LCB: Expanding LiveCodeBench to Support Multiple Programming Languages for LLM Evaluation
tldr: LiveCodeBench (LCB) is a popular benchmark for evaluating LLMs on code generation, but it currently only supports Python. This paper introduces Multi-LCB, which extends LCB to multiple programming languages, enabling broader, contamination‑aware evaluation of LLMs across diverse coding tasks.
significance: By broadening LCB to multiple languages, Multi‑LCB provides a more comprehensive, language‑agnostic benchmark that can accelerate LLM development and deployment across varied programming ecosystems.

title: Approximation and interactive design with exact 3D elastic curves
headline: Fast, Stable Recovery of 3D Elastic Curves for Interactive Design
tldr: We present an 11-parameter analytic description of 3D elastic curve segments equivalent to the spherical pendulum equation. Our numerically stable algorithm recovers these parameters from a given curve, enabling fast, interactive design of elastic shapes.
significance: This work provides a practical tool for designers and engineers to model and manipulate physically realistic elastic curves in real time.

title: S-Agent: Spatial Tool-Use Elicits Reasoning for Spatial Intelligence
headline: S-Agent: A New Spatial Tool-Use Agent for Continuous 3D Reasoning
tldr: S-Agent introduces a spatial tool-use paradigm that enables reasoning over dynamic, multi-view 3D scenes. By integrating continuous visual streams with large language models, it surpasses static inference methods and opens new possibilities for real-time spatial intelligence.
significance: This work bridges the gap between static visual inference and dynamic spatial reasoning, offering a foundation for advanced real-world applications.

title: Probe-and-Refine Tuning of Repository Guidance for Coding Agents
headline: Enhancing LLM Coding Agents with Repository Guidance via AGENTS.md
tldr: LLM-based coding agents lack repository-level context, such as subsystem locations and test workflows. Engineers use AGENTS.md files to provide this guidance, but recent studies question their effectiveness.
significance: Improving repository guidance could significantly boost the reliability and efficiency of automated coding agents.

title: State estimation of Rayleigh-Bénard convection with reduced-order models
headline: State Estimation of Rayleigh-Bénard Convection via Reduced-Order Models and EKF
tldr: The authors present a framework that couples a stable Galerkin ROM with an extended Kalman filter to estimate the state of 2D Rayleigh-Bénard convection. The ROM is built from controllability modes of the linearised Boussinesq equations, and the approach is validated against DNS.
significance: This work enables accurate, computationally efficient state estimation for turbulent convection, facilitating real-time monitoring and control.

title: Efficient and Sound Probabilistic Verification for AI Agents
headline: Probabilistic Verification for AI Agents: Efficient Runtime Monitoring with Datalog Policies
tldr: This work introduces a sound and efficient probabilistic verification framework for AI agents operating in complex digital environments. By extending Datalog-based runtime monitoring to handle probabilistic policies, the approach enables real-time enforcement of security constraints while maintaining computational tractability.
significance: It bridges the gap between deterministic policy enforcement and the stochastic nature of modern AI systems, providing a scalable solution for secure deployment.

title: Planar constant piecewise smooth vector fields with large hysteresis
headline: Rigorous Analysis of Planar Piecewise Smooth Vector Fields Reveals Limit Set Dynamics with Hysteresis
tldr: We provide a rigorous mathematical framework for planar constant piecewise smooth vector fields exhibiting large hysteresis. The study characterizes possible limit sets, offering a foundation for control system analysis.
significance: This work establishes a consistent theoretical basis for predicting complex behaviors in widely used control systems.

title: What Do Safety-Aligned LLMs Learn From Mixed Compliance Demonstrations?
headline: Safety-Aligned LLMs Learn Mixed Compliance: How Benign and Harmful Demonstrations Shape Responses
tldr: The study investigates how language models interpret a blend of benign and harmful compliance demonstrations. Results suggest that mixed demonstrations influence model behavior, revealing nuanced compliance dynamics.
significance: Understanding this interplay informs safer LLM deployment and mitigates jailbreak risks.

title: FreeStyle: Free Control of Style-Content Dual-Reference Generation from Community LoRA Mining
headline: FreeStyle: Community LoRA Mining Enables Precise Style-Content Dual-Reference Generation
tldr: FreeStyle introduces a framework that leverages community-sourced LoRA adapters to flexibly blend a content image with a separate style reference, while maintaining instruction compliance and preventing style leakage. The approach demonstrates state‑of‑the‑art fidelity across diverse datasets and offers an open‑source benchmark for future research.
significance: It establishes a scalable, open‑source pipeline that improves control over diffusion models, bridging the gap between content fidelity and style transfer.

title: Entropy Estimation in Multi-Qutrit Systems via Variational and Classical Neural Networks
headline: Estimating von Neumann Entropy in Multi-Qutrit Systems with Variational and Classical Neural Networks
tldr: We compare variational quantum algorithms and classical CNNs for estimating von Neumann entropy in up to three qutrits, demonstrating that hardware-efficient SU(3)-inspired ansatzes achieve accurate results on an ideal simulator. The study provides a benchmark for future noisy implementations and highlights the complementary strengths of quantum and classical approaches.
significance: This work establishes a systematic benchmark for entropy estimation in multi-qutrit systems, informing both quantum algorithm design and classical machine learning strategies.

title: General circuit mapping algorithm for neutral atom quantum computers
headline: Graph-Theoretic Circuit Mapping Algorithm Enhances Neutral Atom Quantum Computer Performance
tldr: We introduce a graph-theoretic framework that maps quantum circuits onto neutral atom architectures without relying on specific circuit structures. This approach optimizes qubit movement, reducing overhead and improving scalability.
significance: By decoupling compilation from circuit specifics, the method accelerates deployment of complex quantum algorithms on scalable neutral atom platforms.

title: Predictability as a Fine-Grained Measure for Privacy
headline: Fine-Grained Privacy via Predictability: A New Framework Beyond Worst-Case Differential Privacy
tldr: The paper introduces a privacy framework that measures privacy based on predictability, taking into account the attacker's knowledge and a stochastic compromise of the dataset. This approach offers a more nuanced tradeoff between privacy and accuracy compared to traditional worst-case differential privacy.
significance: It provides a more realistic and flexible privacy guarantee that can reduce unnecessary data distortion while still protecting individuals.

title: Caching for Dollars, Not Hits: An Exact Offline Reference for Cloud-Egress Caching and the Crossover That Decides When It Pays
headline: Optimizing Cloud Egress Costs: When Caching Beats Hit Rate for Large Datasets
tldr: This paper provides an exact offline benchmark for cloud‑egress caching, showing that minimizing miss rate is not the right objective when costs are driven by request and byte charges. It identifies the crossover point where caching becomes cost‑effective and offers a reference for evaluating caching strategies.
significance: It redefines caching optimization by focusing on monetary savings rather than hit ratios, guiding practitioners on when to invest in cache infrastructure.

title: How Do Instructions Shape Speech? Cross-Attention Attribution for Style-Captioned Text-to-Speech
headline: Unveiling How Words Shape Voice: Cross-Attention Attribution in Style-Captioned TTS
tldr: We adapt the DAAM framework to speech diffusion models, enabling fine-grained attribution of how individual words influence acoustic output in style-captioned TTS. This insight helps diagnose failures and improve controllability in expressive speech synthesis.
significance: By revealing the word-level influence on voice characteristics, this work advances transparency and control in expressive TTS systems.

title: VisDom: Sparse Novel View Synthesis with Visible Domain Constraint
headline: VisDom: Enhancing Sparse Novel View Synthesis via Visible Domain Constraints
tldr: VisDom introduces a visible domain constraint to improve 3D reconstruction from few views, reducing floating artifacts and geometry inconsistencies. The method outperforms NeRF and Gaussian Splatting baselines on sparse datasets while maintaining realistic renderings.
significance: By enforcing visible domain consistency, VisDom addresses a key limitation of sparse novel view synthesis, enabling more reliable 3D geometry recovery.

title: LedgerAgent: Structured State for Policy-Adherent Tool-Calling Agents
headline: LedgerAgent: Structured State Management for Policy‑Adherent Tool‑Calling Agents in Customer Service Domains
tldr: LedgerAgent introduces a dedicated task state representation that tracks facts, identifiers, constraints, and conditions across turns, enabling policy‑compliant tool calls. By separating state from observations and tool outputs, the agent can enforce domain policies and improve reliability in customer‑service interactions.
significance: This approach bridges the gap between LLMs and structured policy enforcement, offering a scalable framework for real‑time, policy‑aware customer‑service agents.

title: StylisticBias: A Few Human Visual Cues Drive Most Social Biases in MLLMs
headline: StylisticBias Reveals How Visual Cues Shape Social Bias in Multimodal LLMs
tldr: The StylisticBias benchmark isolates visual style from identity to test social bias in multimodal large language models. Results show that a handful of visual cues drive most bias, underscoring the need for targeted mitigation.
significance: Understanding which visual cues cause bias enables more effective debiasing of multimodal LLMs.

title: The $B^+ \to K^+ ν\bar ν$ decay as a QCD axion search: comparing reinterpretation approaches
headline: Belle II B+→K+νν Decay Tightens Constraints on Light Axion Models
tldr: Two independent Belle II analyses of B+→K+νν data give axion limits that differ by a factor of four, traced to the choice of kinematic variable space. The study shows that the resolution in the reconstructed di‑neutrino invariant mass q^2 is the key figure of merit.
significance: By pinpointing the source of discrepancy, the paper refines the interpretation of rare B‑decay searches, sharpening the reach for QCD axion and other invisible particles.

title: String Axiverse Enhancement of Superradiant Dark Matter Production
headline: String Axiverse Boosts Superradiant Dark Matter Production from Primordial Black Holes
tldr: We examine how string theory predicts many light axion species that can be emitted by evaporating primordial black holes, enhancing superradiant amplification. The resulting increase in axion production could significantly affect dark matter abundance and observable signatures.
significance: This study links high-energy string theory predictions with astrophysical processes, offering a new avenue to probe dark matter through primordial black hole dynamics.

title: SARLO-80: Worldwide Slant SAR Language Optic Dataset 80cm
headline: SARLO-80: Global 80cm Slant SAR–Optical Dataset Benchmark for Multimodal AI
tldr: SARLO-80 provides the first high‑resolution (80 cm) slant‑range SAR–optical paired dataset, preserving complex values and native geometry. This enables training of multimodal foundation models that can learn physically grounded cross‑modal representations for remote‑sensing tasks.
significance: By delivering a large, high‑resolution, geometry‑aware SAR–optical benchmark, SARLO‑80 unlocks the development of more accurate, physics‑aware AI for Earth observation.

title: Transfer-matrix functions for algebraically decaying interactions in variational infinite matrix product states
headline: New Transfer-Matrix Approach Eliminates Surrogate Residuals in Algebraic Interaction iMPS
tldr: The authors present a fixed‑D variational energy formulation that directly incorporates algebraically decaying interactions via transfer‑matrix functions, bypassing the need for finite‑pole surrogate Hamiltonians. This eliminates representation residuals and enhances the accuracy of infinite matrix product state calculations.
significance: By removing the surrogate approximation, the method enables more precise simulations of long‑range interacting quantum systems.

title: HumanScale: Egocentric Human Video Can Outperform Real-Robot Data for Embodied Pretraining
headline: Human-Scale Egocentric Video Beats Real-Robot Data for Embodied AI Pretraining
tldr: Egocentric human videos can provide richer, more scalable data for embodied foundation models than teleoperated robot data, achieving comparable or better performance while reducing cost and complexity. This suggests a shift toward large-scale human video datasets for embodied AI.
significance: This work demonstrates that large-scale human egocentric video can replace costly robot data, accelerating embodied AI development.

title: FlowEdit: Associative Memory for Lifelong Pronunciation Adaptation in Flow-Matching TTS
headline: FlowEdit: Lifelong Pronunciation Adaptation for Frozen Flow-Matching TTS via Associative Memory
tldr: FlowEdit introduces a lightweight, lifelong adaptation framework that updates pronunciation in frozen flow-matching TTS models by learning latent conditioning edits instead of retraining weights. This approach corrects out-of-vocabulary proper noun errors on the fly, preserving zero-shot quality while enabling continuous improvement.
significance: By enabling real-time pronunciation corrections without model retraining, FlowEdit extends the practical utility of state-of-the-art flow-matching TTS for dynamic, real-world applications.

title: Thinking in Boxes: 3D Editing in Real Images Made Easy
headline: 3D Boxes Empower Precise Spatial Editing in Real-World Images for Photographers
tldr: This work introduces a method that uses 3D bounding boxes as explicit transformation specifications, enabling accurate edits of large object motions and camera changes in real images. By integrating structured 3D priors into diffusion-based editing pipelines, the approach achieves more reliable spatial control compared to previous weak 2D-conditioning techniques.
significance: It bridges the gap between 2D image editing and 3D spatial reasoning, offering a practical tool for designers and researchers to perform precise, large-scale edits in realistic scenes.
